member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.AIProviderType
member.summary: The AI providers/model runtimes that WEB6 can route a completion request to.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.AIProviderType.OpenServ
member.summary: OpenServ SERV inference gateway - one API key (SERV_API_KEY) reaches every model in the SERV catalog (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, Qwen, DeepSeek) via an OpenAI-compatible chat/completions endpoint. See https://docs.openserv.ai/serv-reasoning/sdk-integration

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.DispatchMode
member.summary: The dispatch mode used by the FAHRN (Fractal Adaptive Holonic Reasoning Network) controller agent.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.DispatchMode.Serial
member.summary: Dispatch to the single highest-scoring agent, falling over to the next best on stall/timeout. Cost optimised.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.DispatchMode.Parallel
member.summary: Dispatch the same problem to every eligible agent in parallel and compare/merge their plans. Accuracy optimised.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.DispatchMode.Decomposed
member.summary: Decompose the problem into sub-problems and route each to its best-fit agent, then merge the resulting plans.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.HolonicMemoryLevel
member.summary: The fractal Holonic BRAID memory hierarchy, from the atomic Session holon up to the planetary Earth holon. Every level is simultaneously a whole in itself and a part of the level above it (Koestler's holon).

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.OrchestratorProtocolType
member.summary: The agent/orchestration protocols and frameworks WEB6 normalises over, so a caller can write once against the WEB6 protocol and reach every agent framework without learning each one's native wire format.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.OrchestratorProtocolType.MCP
member.summary: Model Context Protocol (Anthropic) - tool/resource invocation over JSON-RPC.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.OrchestratorProtocolType.A2A
member.summary: Agent2Agent protocol - cross-vendor agent-to-agent task delegation.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.OrchestratorProtocolType.Webhook
member.summary: A generic JSON-over-HTTP webhook for any orchestrator that doesn't have a dedicated adapter yet.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.RetentionPolicy
member.summary: How long a piece of memory is retained at a given holon before being discarded.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.AIProviderManager
member.summary: The WEB6 AI abstraction layer. Normalises completion requests/responses across every AI provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama, Groq, Mistral, OpenServ, etc.) behind a single unified interface, with automatic provider selection, fail-over and cost/latency/quality-based routing.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.AIProviderManager.ApiKeys
member.summary: API keys per provider. Defaults to reading from environment variables (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY) if not supplied.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.AIProviderManager.CompleteAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionRequest)
member.summary: Routes a unified completion request to the best-fit provider (or the one explicitly requested), normalising the response shape. Automatically fails over to the next provider on error if request.Routing.Fallback is true.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.AIProviderManager.CallAWSBedrockAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionRequest)
member.summary: Calls AWS Bedrock's unified Converse API - the same call shape works across every model Bedrock hosts (Anthropic Claude, Amazon Titan/Nova, Meta Llama, Mistral, etc.). Credentials are resolved via the standard AWS SDK credential chain (environment variables, shared config/profile, or an IAM role) - set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY/AWS_REGION (or AWS_DEFAULT_REGION) to use this provider.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.AIProviderManager.GenerateImageAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ImageGenerationRequest)
member.summary: Generates an image via the configured image-generation provider. Stability AI is fully wired (real REST call to the Stable Image Generate v2beta API). OpenAI's gpt-image-1 is wired as a second option.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager
member.summary: FAHRN - the Fractal Adaptive Holonic Reasoning Network. The meta-orchestration controller agent that sits above the reasoning network: it classifies problems, scores every registered agent via a composite score (category performance, speed, cost, loop-detection stability, failure rate), dispatches work in Serial, Parallel or Decomposed mode, runs loop detection, and learns from every outcome via EMA score updates that are persisted as ReasoningAgent holons in the Holonic BRAID memory hierarchy.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager.EMAAlpha
member.summary: The smoothing factor for the Exponential Moving Average used to update agent scores after every task.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager.RegisterAgentAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ReasoningAgentMetadata)
member.summary: Registers a new reasoning agent with the network, persisted as a ReasoningAgent holon.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager.SeedDefaultOpenServAgentsAsync
member.summary: Registers one reasoning agent per model in the OpenServ SERV catalog (skipping any AgentName already registered), so FAHRN can immediately score, dispatch and braid across every OpenServ-reachable model (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, Qwen, DeepSeek) behind the single SERV_API_KEY. Safe to call repeatedly.

member.name: F:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager.OpenServSeedAgents
member.summary: Default scoring metadata for one reasoning agent per model in . Speed/cost/category scores are seeded estimates (0-1, higher is better/cheaper/faster) intended as a reasonable starting point - FAHRN's EMA updates (see ) adjust them toward real observed behaviour as dispatches complete.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager.GetRegisteredAgentsAsync
member.summary: Loads every agent currently registered with the network.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager.DispatchAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.DispatchRequest)
member.summary: The controller agent's dispatch logic: scores every eligible agent for the given task type, picks a dispatch mode (Serial/Parallel/Decomposed), executes the agent(s), runs loop detection, assembles the final Mermaid execution plan, persists/reuses the Holonic BRAID graph for the task type, and updates every involved agent's score via EMA before returning.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.FAHRNManager.DetectLoop(System.String)
member.summary: Loop detection: flags output-similarity (degenerate repetition) and circular DAG references in the Mermaid diagram. Token-budget and self-contradictory-step detection are evaluated by the caller against the raw provider response before this is called.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicBraidManager
member.summary: Manages the Holonic BRAID shared reasoning-graph library (whitepaper §3). The library is itself a holon (HolonicBraidLibrary) - its children are individual graph holons (HolonicBraidGraph), one per task type τ. Every Mermaid reasoning graph generated by any agent, for any task type, is stored once and re-used by every future agent/session that hits the same task type - so PPD (performance-per-dollar) gains compound globally instead of resetting per agent/instance. Graphs are saved through the OASIS COSMIC ORM, so they automatically replicate/fail-over across whichever providers (MongoDB for fast access, Solana for immutable on-chain provenance, IPFS for decentralised permanence, etc.) are configured for auto-replication in OASIS_DNA.json - the same holon Id resolves to the same graph from any backend (ProviderUniqueStorageKey, per provider).

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicBraidManager.AccuracyRegenerationThreshold
member.summary: If a graph's average solver accuracy drops below this threshold, the Generator is reinvoked to produce an improved replacement.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicBraidManager.EnsureLibraryHolonAsync
member.summary: Gets the single shared library holon, creating it if this is the very first call anywhere.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicBraidManager.FindGraphForTaskTypeAsync(System.String)
member.summary: The lookup-or-create entry point: finds an existing reasoning graph for the given task type in the shared library. Returns null if none exists yet (the caller's generator agent should then create one and call SaveGraphAsync). Every successful lookup is at zero generation cost - this is what sustains BRAID's PPD gains at platform scale instead of collapsing as task volume grows (whitepaper §2.4).

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicBraidManager.SaveGraphAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Stores a newly generated reasoning graph as a child of the shared library holon (called once per task type by the generator agent, or to publish a new version when regenerating a degraded/conflicting graph).

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicBraidManager.RecordSolverOutcomeAsync(System.Guid,System.Boolean,System.Double)
member.summary: Feeds a real solver outcome back into a graph's quality metadata via EMA, so the library's accuracy tracking stays current (whitepaper §3.1 - "usage_count, avg_solver_accuracy" accumulate over time).

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicMemoryManager
member.summary: Manages the Holonic BRAID fractal memory hierarchy - Session → Agent → User → Group → Neighbourhood → District → City → County → Country → Continent → Earth. Every level is a holon that is simultaneously a whole in itself and a part of the level above it. Membrane rules (per-field, consent-governed) decide exactly what memory is allowed to propagate from a child holon up to its parent - the default is private; nothing propagates without an explicit rule. This is how genuine bottom-up collective intelligence forms without becoming surveillance.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicMemoryManager.GetOrCreateEarthHolonAsync
member.summary: Gets the single planetary Earth holon, creating it if this is the very first call anywhere.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicMemoryManager.GetOrCreateHolonAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Enums.HolonicMemoryLevel,System.String,System.Guid)
member.summary: Finds an existing holon at the given level/name/parent, or creates a new one.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicMemoryManager.SetMembraneRuleAsync(System.Guid,NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.MembraneRule)
member.summary: Sets the membrane rule that governs what this holon is allowed to propagate upward to its parent.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicMemoryManager.RecordMemoryAsync(System.Guid,NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicMemoryItem)
member.summary: Records a new memory item at the given holon. Does not propagate it anywhere by itself - call PropagateAsync for that.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.HolonicMemoryManager.PropagateAsync(System.Guid)
member.summary: Propagates whatever memory items are permitted by the child holon's membrane rule up to its parent holon (a single hop - call again at the parent level to continue propagation further up the hierarchy). The default is private: any field not explicitly listed in FieldsAllowedToPropagate is never propagated.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.OrchestratorManager
member.summary: Abstracts and aggregates every agent protocol/orchestration framework (MCP, A2A, LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, Semantic Kernel, or any generic webhook-based orchestrator) behind one unified WEB6 interface. Register an external orchestrator endpoint once via RegisterAdapterAsync, then invoke it through InvokeAsync using the same normalised request/response shape regardless of which protocol it actually speaks underneath - the same "one API, every AI" abstraction the AIProviderManager provides for raw model providers, extended to cover entire orchestration frameworks and multi-agent protocols.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.OrchestratorManager.RegisterAdapterAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorAdapterConfig)
member.summary: Registers an external agent/orchestrator endpoint, persisted as an OrchestratorAdapter holon.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.OrchestratorManager.GetAdaptersAsync
member.summary: Lists every registered orchestrator adapter.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.OrchestratorManager.InvokeAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorInvokeRequest)
member.summary: Invokes a registered orchestrator adapter with a normalised request, translating to/from its native protocol wire format under the hood, so every caller deals with the same WEB6 shape regardless of whether the adapter underneath is an MCP server, an A2A agent, a LangChain/AutoGen/CrewAI/Semantic Kernel deployment, or a plain webhook.

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.OrchestratorManager.InvokeJsonWebhookAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorAdapterConfig,NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorInvokeRequest,System.String)
member.summary: Generic JSON-over-HTTP invocation for protocols whose wire format is a simple {fieldName, parameters} POST body (covers LangChain/AutoGen/CrewAI/Semantic Kernel/A2A REST bridges and plain webhooks).

member.name: M:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Managers.OrchestratorManager.InvokeMcpAsync(NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorAdapterConfig,NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorInvokeRequest)
member.summary: Invokes an MCP server's tools/call JSON-RPC method - the tool name comes from ExtraConfig["tool"].

Performs the full MCP Streamable HTTP transport handshake - initialize, then the notifications/initialized notification, then the actual tools/call - carrying the server-issued Mcp-Session-Id across all three requests (per the MCP spec, a server may require this session to be established before accepting calls).

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ChatMessage
member.summary: A single message in a chat-style completion request, normalised across every AI provider.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ChatMessage.Role
member.summary: "system", "user" or "assistant".

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionRequest
member.summary: A unified completion request - the single request shape WEB6 accepts for every AI provider. POST https://api.web6.oasisomniverse.one/v1/complete

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionRequest.Provider
member.summary: "auto", "openai", "anthropic", "gemini", "ollama", etc. "auto" lets WEB6 pick the best provider.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionRequest.Model
member.summary: "auto" or a specific model id (e.g. "gpt-4o", "claude-opus-4-8").

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionRequest.AvatarId
member.summary: Optional OASIS avatar id - when set, the request is grounded with WEB4 identity/karma and WEB5 holon/STARNET context.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionResponse
member.summary: The unified completion response returned for every AI provider WEB6 routes to.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.CompletionResponse.FailedOver
member.summary: True if WEB6 had to fail over to a different provider/model than requested.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.DispatchRequest
member.summary: A request to the FAHRN controller agent to dispatch a problem to the reasoning network.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.DispatchRequest.TaskType
member.summary: Task category used for composite scoring, e.g. "mathematics", "legal", "code", "writing", "real-time".

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.DispatchRequest.EligibleAgentIds
member.summary: If empty, every registered agent is eligible.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.AgentExecutionPlan
member.summary: A single agent's contribution to a dispatch (its score at dispatch time, its Mermaid execution plan and its outcome).

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.DispatchResult
member.summary: The result of a FAHRN dispatch - the merged/selected execution plan plus every agent's contribution.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.DispatchResult.FinalMermaidPlan
member.summary: Final assembled Mermaid plan - selected (serial), merged (parallel) or stitched (decomposed).

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.DispatchResult.HolonicBraidGraphId
member.summary: The id of the HolonicBraidGraph holon this plan was loaded from or saved as, for the given TaskType.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicBraidGraphDto
member.summary: A reasoning graph in the Holonic BRAID shared library - a Mermaid execution graph generated once per task type by a high-tier "generator" agent, then re-used (and executed cheaply) by every future agent/session doing the same task type. Stored as a HolonicBraidGraph holon so it persists/replicates across every OASIS provider.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicBraidGraphDto.ParentHolonId
member.summary: The Library holon this graph holon is a child of (per whitepaper §3.1 - the library holon is itself a holon whose children are individual graph holons, one per task type).

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicBraidGraphDto.AvgSolverAccuracy
member.summary: Average solver accuracy reported back from real outcomes (0-1). Drives accuracy-threshold regeneration.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicBraidGraphDto.Version
member.summary: Bumped every time a new version of this graph replaces a previous one (accuracy regeneration or conflict resolution).

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicMemoryItem
member.summary: A single piece of memory recorded at a holon (e.g. a session fact, a performance signal, a topic).

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicMemoryItem.Tags
member.summary: Tags used to evaluate membrane TriggerConditions, e.g. "topic:professional", "anonymised".

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.HolonicMemoryHolonDto
member.summary: A holon in the Holonic BRAID fractal memory hierarchy (Session → Agent → User → Group → ... → Earth).

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ImageGenerationRequest
member.summary: A unified image-generation request - the image-modality counterpart to CompletionRequest.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ImageGenerationRequest.Provider
member.summary: Which image-generation provider to use. Defaults to StabilityAI.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ImageGenerationRequest.Model
member.summary: "auto" or a specific model id.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ImageGenerationRequest.Size
member.summary: OpenAI-style size, e.g. "1024x1024".

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ImageGenerationRequest.AspectRatio
member.summary: Stability AI-style aspect ratio, e.g. "16:9".

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ImageGenerationResponse
member.summary: A unified image-generation response.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.MembraneRule
member.summary: The governed boundary through which information passes between two holons in the Holonic BRAID hierarchy. The default is private - nothing propagates without an explicit rule. Per-field granularity, matching the OASIS WEB4 field-level data control system.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.MembraneRule.FieldsAllowedToPropagate
member.summary: The field/topic/knowledge-category names from the child holon that are allowed to flow upward to the parent.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.MembraneRule.Retention
member.summary: How long the propagated memory is retained at the parent holon.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.MembraneRule.WhoCanRead
member.summary: Holon ids (parents, siblings or external agents) explicitly permitted to query/read this holon's propagated memory. Read access is separate from propagation.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.MembraneRule.TriggerCondition
member.summary: Optional conditional trigger, e.g. "topic=professional" or "anonymised-aggregate-only". Evaluated against the memory item's own tags before it is allowed to propagate - if empty, the rule applies unconditionally.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.MembraneRule.AnonymisedAggregateOnly
member.summary: True if only anonymised/aggregate patterns (not raw content) may propagate under this rule.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OpenServModel
member.summary: One model entry in the OpenServ SERV inference catalog.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OpenServCatalog
member.summary: The SERV catalog reachable through OpenServ's OpenAI-compatible chat/completions endpoint with a single SERV_API_KEY - spans every underlying provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI, Qwen, DeepSeek). Kept in sync with the OASIS IDE's OPENSERV_MODELS list (src/main/services/OpenServAgentService.ts). See https://docs.openserv.ai/serv-reasoning/sdk-integration and https://docs.openserv.ai/serv-reasoning/models

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorAdapterConfig
member.summary: A registered external agent/orchestrator endpoint (an MCP server, an A2A agent, a LangChain/AutoGen/CrewAI/ Semantic Kernel deployment, or any generic webhook) that WEB6 will normalise traffic to/from.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorAdapterConfig.EndpointUrl
member.summary: The base URL of the external orchestrator/agent endpoint.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorAdapterConfig.AuthToken
member.summary: Optional bearer token / API key for the external endpoint.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorAdapterConfig.ExtraConfig
member.summary: Arbitrary extra headers/config the specific protocol adapter needs (e.g. MCP tool name).

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorInvokeRequest
member.summary: A normalised request sent through an orchestrator adapter - the same CompletionRequest shape used by every other AI provider.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.OrchestratorInvokeResponse
member.summary: A normalised response from an orchestrator adapter, regardless of the underlying protocol's native wire format.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ReasoningAgentMetadata
member.summary: The continuously-updated scoring metadata for a single reasoning agent registered with FAHRN (the Fractal Adaptive Holonic Reasoning Network controller). Persisted as a ReasoningAgent holon.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ReasoningAgentMetadata.CategoryScores
member.summary: Per-category score (0-1), e.g. "mathematics", "legal", "architecture", "code", "writing", "real-time".

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ReasoningAgentMetadata.SpeedScore
member.summary: 0-1, higher is faster.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ReasoningAgentMetadata.CostScore
member.summary: 0-1, higher is cheaper.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ReasoningAgentMetadata.LoopDetectionScore
member.summary: 0-1, higher is more stable / less prone to looping.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ReasoningAgentMetadata.FailureRate
member.summary: 0-1, fraction of recent tasks this agent has failed/stalled on.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.RoutingOptions
member.summary: Routing preferences for a WEB6 completion request.

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.RoutingOptions.Priority
member.summary: "cost", "latency" or "quality".

member.name: P:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.RoutingOptions.Fallback
member.summary: Whether to automatically fail over to the next-best provider if the chosen one errors/times out.

member.name: T:NextGenSoftware.OASIS.Web6.Core.Models.ScoringWeights
member.summary: The composite routing score weights for a given dispatch mode. CompositeScore = (Wcat * CategoryScore) + (Wspeed * SpeedScore) + (Wcost * CostScore) - (Wpenalty * FailureRate) - (LoopPenalty if recently stalled)
